params.text: Price equilibrium is the point where market supply and demand meet, determining a stable market price and quantity.

narration: Price equilibrium is the point where market supply and demand meet, determining a stable market price and quantity.
First, we have the supply curve, showing how much producers are willing to sell at various prices.
Then, the demand curve illustrates how much consumers are willing to buy at different price points.

params.text: The intersection of these two curves represents the market's equilibrium point.

narration: The intersection of these two curves represents the market's equilibrium point.
At this equilibrium, the quantity supplied perfectly matches the quantity demanded, clearing the market.

params.text: This point defines the equilibrium price (P*) and equilibrium quantity (Q*), where market forces are balanced.

narration: This point defines the equilibrium price (P*) and equilibrium quantity (Q*), where market forces are balanced.
